req.title: Card PIN change (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window. Test design applies: EP for a valid single change in the allowed partition; BVA for the exact boundary at attempt 5 (last allowed) and attempt 6 (first rejected); NEG for exceeding the limit; STC for the state transition from 'limit not reached' to 'limit reached'; DT for the decision of allow vs. reject based on attempt count. Each test resets state via a dedicated fixture that clears rate-limit counters before execution, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_pin_change_single_valid_request_succeeds
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated caller
Rate-limit counter for card is reset to 0 (no prior changes in window)
actions: Send PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: EP: single request is within the allowed equivalence partition (1..5)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pin_change_single_valid_request_succeeds(api_client, card_with_reset_limit):
    card_id = card_with_reset_limit["id"]
    payload = {"pin": "1234"}

    response = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "updated"

tests.name: test_pin_change_sixth_attempt_returns_429
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated caller
Rate-limit counter for card is pre-seeded to 5 (limit already exhausted)
actions: Send PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN as the 6th attempt
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains rate-limit error message
trace: NEG: request beyond allowed partition triggers error response
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pin_change_sixth_attempt_returns_429(api_client, card_with_exhausted_limit):
    card_id = card_with_exhausted_limit["id"]
    payload = {"pin": "5678"}

    response = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 429
    body = response.json()
    assert "rate" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "limit" in body.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_pin_change_fifth_attempt_is_allowed_sixth_is_rejected
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated caller
Rate-limit counter for card is pre-seeded to 4 (four prior changes in window)
actions: Send PATCH /cards/{id}/pin — 6th attempt (boundary max+1, first rejected)
expected: 5th attempt returns HTTP 200 and PIN updated
6th attempt returns HTTP 429 rate limit error
trace: R-FIN-047: at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window
BVA: test at exact upper boundary (5) and one above boundary (6)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_pin_change_fifth_attempt_is_allowed_sixth_is_rejected(
    api_client, card_with_four_prior_changes
):
    card_id = card_with_four_prior_changes["id"]

    r5 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": "2468"})
    assert r5.status_code == 200, f"5th attempt failed: {r5.text}"
    assert r5.json().get("status") == "updated"

    r6 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/pin", json={"pin": "1357"})
    assert r6.status_code == 429, f"6th attempt should be rejected: {r6.text}"
    body6 = r6.json()
    assert "rate" in body6.get("error", "").lower() or "limit" in body6.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_pin_change_transitions_state_from_open_to_rate_limited
preconditions: Card belongs to the authenticated caller
Rate-limit counter for card is reset to 0
actions: Send 5 sequential PATCH /cards/{id}/pin requests and collect all responses
expected: State has transitioned from OPEN to RATE_LIMITED after 5th change
trace: R-FIN-047: at most 5 PIN changes per 24-hour window
STC: covers state transition from OPEN (changes allowed) to RATE_LIMITED (changes blocked)

tests.name: test_pin_change_rejected_when_card_does_not_belong_to_caller
preconditions: Card ID belongs to a different customer (not the caller)
Rate-limit counter is irrelevant — authorization check fires first
actions: Send PATCH /cards/{other_card_id}/pin with a valid new PIN
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 403 or 404 returned (card does not belong to caller)
trace: R-FIN-047 precondition: card belongs to caller
DT: decision row where ownership=false and attempts=any => deny access
